seoTitle: How to Copy a Report Page to Another Power BI File
metaDescription: Migrate an entire Power BI report page into a different file using the multi-instance clipboard, without rebuilding every chart and DAX formula.
intro: Rebuilding every chart visual and DAX formula from scratch just to move a styled dashboard page into a new file wastes hours. Power BI supports copying entire report page frames between open project files using its multi-instance clipboard architecture. This guide covers launching both files and transferring the page cleanly.

steps.heading: Open both project files
steps.body: Launch concurrent Power BI Desktop instances for both your source file and the target `.pbix` file you want to copy the page into.

steps.heading: Copy the page from the lower menu bar
steps.body: Right-click the page tab at the bottom of the source file and copy the entire tab.

steps.heading: Paste into the target file
steps.body: Switch to the target file's window and paste the page, which transfers the visual configurations and page state.

steps.heading: Match missing relationship keys
steps.body: Check whether the target file's data model has matching tables and relationships, since copying a page requires the underlying data structures to align.

steps.heading: Resolve display placeholders
steps.body: Address any visuals showing placeholder errors due to missing fields, which usually means the target model needs the same table or column added.

faq.question: Can I copy a page between two completely different data models?
faq.answer: You can, but any visuals relying on fields not present in the target model will show placeholder errors until you add matching data structures.

faq.question: Do I need both files open at the same time?
faq.answer: Yes, the cross-instance copy and paste process requires launching both the source and target files simultaneously.

faq.question: Does copying a page bring over the theme and styling?
faq.answer: Yes, the multi-instance clipboard serializes visual configurations and page states, including styling.

faq.question: Why are some visuals broken after pasting into the new file?
faq.answer: This typically happens when the target file's data model is missing tables, columns, or relationships that the copied visuals depend on.
